home.description: Here you will find support and guidance.
home.intro_text: I am Melisa Tsela, a licensed Psychologist specializing in Systemic Psychotherapy and Counseling. In systemic psychotherapy, we approach each person within the context of their relationships, interactions, and roles. Through conversation, as well as experiential tools that always follow your own pace, we unfold the patterns that repeat in your life. Our goal is to help you view your difficulties from a new perspective and discover new communication and behavior patterns that can bring balance not only to you, but also to your relationships.

services.individual_desc: A personal space to share your thoughts and work together on issues of anxiety, depression, or self-improvement.
services.online_desc: Distance is not an obstacle. We conduct sessions via secure video conferencing platforms from the comfort of your home.
services.growth_desc: Focus on self-awareness and developing new skills for a more balanced and meaningful life.

process.subtitle: The Process
process.title: How We Will Work Together
process.step1_desc: You contact me via email, phone, or message to share your request. Deciding to take the first step is enough.
process.step2_desc: We meet online in a safe and private digital space. It is our opportunity to get to know each other, see whether you feel comfortable, and discuss how I can support you.
process.step3_desc: We begin our therapeutic journey with small, steady steps.

bio.hero_description:
From a very early age, even back in my school years, I realized the power of deep, meaningful listening. Observing how rare it is to find someone who listens with genuine attention and without judgment, I decided to turn this need into my profession.

I believe that psychotherapy should not be a 'strict' or judgmental process. Having been in the client's seat myself, I understood that true change does not come through pressure, but through understanding and acceptance. For me, therapy is the creation of a safe space where you can-perhaps for the first time-hear your own voice and feel that someone is truly present, there to understand you.

In our sessions, my goal is not to offer ready-made answers or quick fixes, but to provide a secure environment that will help you reflect and see your story through a different lens. Ultimately, psychotherapy is an act of self-care, in a setting where it is finally permitted not to be 'perfect'.
bio.approach_text: I believe that the challenges we face are not flaws in our character, but the ways we learned to survive within our relationships, family, work, and everyday life.
bio.edu_uni: University of Ioannina - Department of Psychology
bio.edu_systemic: Ke.S.Me.Th. - Four-year Specialization in Systemic Psychotherapy and Counseling (E.F.T.A. & E.E.P.E.)
bio.edu_psychoanalysis: DI.KE.PSY. - Annual program 'Psychoanalytic Thinking and Practice'
bio.edu_narrative: Institute 'Logos Psychis' - Narrative tools and techniques in therapeutic practice
bio.exp_center: Private Practice - Psychologist, individual online psychological support sessions
bio.exp_volunteer: Municipal Clinics of the City of Athens - Psychologist, individual in-person psychological support sessions
bio.exp_practice: Athens Child Mental Health Center - Internship
bio.systemic_desc: It approaches the client through their family and close interpersonal relationships. Difficulties are resolved through the restructuring of relational patterns that we automatically adopt in our daily lives.
bio.systemic_tech_desc: The frequency is usually weekly. Although the process is primarily individual, joint meetings with members of the environment may be proposed if deemed necessary.
bio.quality_note: To ensure the quality of my therapeutic work, I am in continuous supervision and personal therapy.

contact.hero_subtitle: I am here to listen to whatever concerns you and support you on your journey.
contact.booking_desc: Check availability and book your online session instantly via Calendly.
contact.form_desc: If you prefer, fill out the form below and I will get back to you as soon as possible.
contact.online_notice: All sessions are held exclusively via video call (Zoom or Viber), ensuring flexibility and privacy.

blog.title: Our Blog
blog.subtitle: Discover articles about psychology and personal growth
blog.author: Author

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro_text: This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal data when you visit our website and use our services.
privacy.data_collection_text: We only collect the necessary data you provide through the contact form (Name, Email) and the Calendly booking system for the purpose of scheduling sessions.
privacy.confidentiality_text: As a psychologist, I strictly adhere to the code of ethics and professional secrecy. Any information shared during sessions remains confidential, except for the exceptions provided by law.
privacy.cookies_text: We use cookies to improve your browsing experience. You can disable cookies through your browser settings.
privacy.rights_text: You have the right to request access to, correction, or deletion of the personal data we hold. For any request, you can contact us via email.

questions.q: How is an online session conducted?
questions.a: At the moment, sessions are conducted via a secure video platform (Zoom or Viber), depending on what works best for you. All you need is a stable internet connection and a quiet space.

questions.q: How can I pay for a session?
questions.a: Payment is made by bank transfer or through IRIS instant payments. Sessions are prepaid in order to confirm your appointment.

Cancellations or changes are accepted up to 24 hours before the scheduled appointment. If canceled in less than 24 hours, the session is charged in full. Exceptions apply for serious health or emergency reasons.

questions.q: Is online psychotherapy as effective as in-person therapy?
questions.a: Absolutely. Research shows that online therapy can be just as effective as in-person therapy, while offering greater flexibility and saving travel time.
Everything we discuss is covered by therapeutic confidentiality. Confidentiality is only lifted in situations involving risk.

questions.q: What will I need to do in the first session?
questions.a: In our first meeting, we will have the opportunity to get to know each other and discuss what brings you to therapy. It is a space for you to see whether you feel comfortable with me and for us to decide how to move forward.

questions.q: How long does each session last?
questions.a: Each individual session lasts 45 to 50 minutes. It is important to be on time so the therapeutic time can be fully used.
